behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is pure key-derivation correctness (determinism + collision properties) at a local function/object boundary, with no required OS/network/subprocess or model dependency in the behavior text. Higher levels were considered and rejected: Integration is disqualified because the behavior text does not require cross-module handoff; System is disqualified because there is no external contract/round-trip side effect requirement; Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI step is present.
behaviors.test_description: Property/unit tests on the key generator: same normalized parameter sets always yield identical keys; varied parameter sets (including ordering, types, nulls, unicode, edge values) do not collide within a large generated corpus.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: This depends on wiring between category configuration and TTL computation, so a multi-module check is the lowest added reality signal beyond unit-only logic tests. System was considered and rejected because the behavior text does not require real external contract checks or external side-effect round-trip. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI output is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that loads real cache freshness configuration used by the app and verifies TTL outputs for categories (e.g., user profiles=300s, product catalog=3600s), including fallback/default category handling and config overrides.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode is mainly orchestration/state handoff across modules (memory monitor + config + strategy selector), which unit-only tests can miss. System was considered and rejected because the behavior text does not explicitly require real OS memory probes or external contract validation; threshold crossing can be injected in-process. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by absence of AI steps.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that drives cache memory-usage metrics across the threshold using in-process components and verifies strategy state transitions from LRU to LFU according to configuration, including hysteresis/boundary values if implemented.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly requires real DB write behavior and proving an externally visible side effect via round-trip (post-write reads observe cache invalidation/refetch), which matches the System triad. Integration was considered but rejected because in-process fakes can hide real invalidation-trigger semantics tied to PostgreSQL behavior/events/transactions.
behaviors.test_description: System test with real PostgreSQL: prime cache via reads, perform a real table write/update, then re-read affected keys through the public data-access path and verify stale cache entries are invalidated (miss + fresh DB-backed value), while unaffected keys remain cached.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on interaction between access tracking state and eviction execution under capacity pressure, which is beyond isolated unit logic in many implementations. System was considered and rejected because behavior text does not require real external protocol/OS/subprocess validation or external side-effect round-trip. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model decision exists.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that fills cache to capacity, applies controlled access patterns, and verifies evicted keys match LRU expectations in LRU mode and LFU expectations in LFU mode, using the real in-process cache stack.
